brief_justification: Arrives at exact correct answer (C) with valid compile-time dependency reasoning; analyzes three levels — compile vs runtime, option-by-option refutation, and architectural fix; acknowledges injection context and flags a common misconception but doesn't name own reasoning biases; verifies by systematic elimination of all four options but no counterfactual or boundary-condition test; acknowledges runtime coupling in D is a real concern but not the core issue, without explicit confidence levels; all four alternatives evaluated with specific rejection reasons; every step traceable — boundary rule stated, import mechanism explained, fix proposed; avoids the anti-pattern of equating interface dependency with no dependency, though doesn't explicitly name the negative gate's community-ontology framing; explicitly applies both suppress signals at the start and proposes a shared-abstractions module matching the 'no shared artefact' suppression; reasoning is largely linear — answer → refute alternatives → fix — without the branch/gate/loop structure the topology prescribes
